untranslatable_reason: This signal requires detection of angled channel patterns with perpendicular width measurement and geometric projection of price targets. These are structural/geometric pattern recognition tasks that require: (1) identification of support/resistance lines at non-horizontal angles, (2) calculation of perpendicular distance between angled lines, (3) projection of that distance from a breakout point. The allowed indicator set contains only price bars, moving averages, and oscillators—no pattern detection, line fitting, or geometric calculation capabilities. Channel detection and width measurement cannot be expressed with the available operators (>, <, >=, <=, ==, crosses_above, crosses_below, is) and indicators.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_3 is a classic technical pattern analysis rule from Perry Kaufman's work on consolidation breakouts. It requires sophisticated pattern recognition beyond the scope of indicator-based rule engines. A proper implementation would need: custom pattern detector for angled channels, linear regression or support/resistance line identification, perpendicular distance calculation, and dynamic target projection. Recommend implementing as a separate pattern scanning module or using a chart pattern recognition library.
